reasoning: The reference value on 2025-04-27 corresponds to the Wednesday, April 23 observation of 73.0 million. This marks the lowest observed value in the past year (matching the Feb 12 value), serving as a localized baseline 'floor' in recent liquidity swap allocations. Historical trends over the last few months show that drops to this floor are typically followed by short-term upward bounces due to flexible 7-day swap demand (e.g., a rapid jump from 73 to >90 in February). Therefore, the near-term probability (May 4) of seeing a value strictly >73.0 is relatively high (~0.65). However, there is a clear, long-term structural downtrend in the baseline (linear trend estimate of -0.606 per day) indicating waning demand for central bank dollar liquidity swaps overall. As we extend the forecast horizon to July and October, the underlying base could decay further (potentially towards zero), meaning that temporary spikes would have to be much higher just to hurdle the 73.0 bar. This implies increased uncertainty and a declining probability of exceedance over time. Following the instruction to have uncertainty increase across the timeline, the forecasts sequentially decay closer to 0.50 (0.65, 0.60, 0.55, 0.51).
